title: muhlenbergia torreyi
intro: Muhlenbergia torreyi, also known as Torrey's muhly or Torrey's hair grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its purple flowers and slender leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Muhlenbergia torreyi include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, and it can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for medicinal use.
